mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that is flat-to-declining over the trailing 3 months signals reduced discounting pressure on equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice future cash flows at lower rates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.64% as of t-1mo, down from prior tightening peak
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trailing-3mo change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or positive-slope periods do not show statistically higher mean returns than flat/rising periods (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades and recession risk pricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: When the 0.3pp trigger fires, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of historical triggered instances, or the effect is not significant at p<0.10 in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation (i.e., real policy rate is meaningfully positive), the joint restrictive stance compresses valuations and forward returns turn negative over 20 trading days; the effect requires both a high nominal rate and low/falling inflation acting together, not either series alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12m CPIAUCSL % change exceeds 1.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns during periods with real rate gap > 1.5pp are not significantly lower than the unconditional sample mean (p>0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS elevated and rising over prior 6 months), a decline in HOUST housing starts signals amplified rate-sensitivity damage to the broader economy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; in easing or neutral regimes the same HOUST decline carries no reliable signal.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1246k units as of t-4mo
failure_condition: If HOUST declines predict negative forward 60d returns with similar magnitude and significance in both tightening and easing regimes, the regime-conditionality is falsified; also fails if Rank IC < 0.03 within the tightening-regime subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO industrial production reflects improving corporate output and near-term earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as the market incorporates the surprise.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM change and forward 5d SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign hit-rate is not above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in RSAFS retail sales growth below trend (falling more than 1 standard deviation from its trailing 12-month average pace) signals weakening consumer demand that precedes downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth falls more than 1 std dev below trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 734967 million as of t-2mo
failure_condition: If triggered instances produce positive forward 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or effect size is not significant at p<0.10 in walk-forward testing, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-style joint signal where UNRATE's 3-month moving average rises 0.5pp above its trailing 12-month low while PAYEMS growth simultaneously turns negative indicates recession onset, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this requires both series moving together, since either alone is noisy.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3mo MA rises >= 0.5pp above trailing 12mo low AND PAYEMS MoM change turns negative
failure_condition: If the joint trigger fires and forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of historical instances, or the interaction effect is no stronger than either individual series' effect (tested via nested model comparison), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% while GDPC1 quarterly growth remains positive, the market interprets continued restrictive policy amid resilient growth as prolonging the tightening cycle, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as short-term rate-sensitive repricing dominates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% while GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth remains positive
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 3.64% as of t-1mo, GDPC1 24026.834 as of t-7mo
regime_hint: restrictive monetary regime with resilient growth
failure_condition: Since FEDFUNDS is currently below the 4.5% threshold, the hypothesis is untestable until triggered; it is falsified if, upon triggering in future data, forward 5d SPX returns are not significantly negative (p>0.10) across at least 5 independent trigger episodes.
